title: Race multi-select cannot mix Unknown / Not-Reported with other choices
description: When RACE is a select_multiple field with Unknown (UNK) and/or Not-Reported (NOT_REP) options, the field gets a constraint preventing mixing those sentinel values with any other selection: `not(selected(.,'UNK') and count-selected(.) > 1) and not(selected(.,'NOT_REP') and count-selected(.) > 1)`. Constraint message clarifies the rule. Option names UNK and NOT_REP are the canonical OC defaults; studies using different option keys for these concepts need study-scope override.
rationale: RACE is a multi-select because subjects can have mixed-race ancestry. Unknown and Not-Reported are sentinel values that semantically exclude selecting any specific race: if the answer is unknown, then no specific race can be reported alongside it. Without the constraint, coordinators can produce nonsensical entries like ['White', 'Unknown'] that complicate downstream demographic analysis.

ensure.field.constraint: not(selected(.,'UNK') and count-selected(.) > 1) and not(selected(.,'NOT_REP') and count-selected(.) > 1)
ensure.field.constraint_message: Cannot select Unknown or Not Reported alongside other race options.

flag.message: RACE multi-select constraint pinned to UNK/NOT_REP exclusivity rule. Verify the field's choices list uses 'UNK' and 'NOT_REP' as the option keys for Unknown and Not-Reported; if the study uses different keys, override this convention at study scope and adjust the constraint expression accordingly.

effect.soft: If the RACE field's choices use different option keys than UNK/NOT_REP (e.g. 'unknown', 'not_reported', 'NOTSPEC'), the constraint expression must reference the actual keys. Option-key-aware constraint generation is study-specific and not yet expressible structurally; ensure provides the canonical default and the flag surfaces this for review.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7I during Phase B.1c-final. Overlap candidate with B.0 inventory §27 (general sentinel_exclusivity_constraint) — when System 2 lands in Phase B.1d, §27 becomes the cascade-resolved general rule and 7I becomes its RACE-specific reaffirmation OR §27 absorbs 7I and 7I retires. Decision deferred to B.1d. Hybrid because the canonical UNK/NOT_REP option keys are assumed; the soft directive carries the override guidance for studies using different keys.

Superseded by §27 (validation.sentinel_value_exclusivity_constraint) imported in Phase B.1d. 7I was RACE-field-specific with the DECLINED sentinel hardcoded as the trigger. §27 generalizes: covers ALL select_multiple fields with ANY recognized sentinel value (DECLINED, UNKNOWN, NONE, N/A, REFUSED — case-insensitive). Crucially §27 explicitly EXCLUDES `OTHER` from sentinel treatment because OTHER semantically combines with real values. The RACE field will still get the constraint under §27 because DECLINED is in its choice list; coverage is preserved and broadened. Cascade resolution skips archived conventions; queries for the sentinel-exclusivity rule will resolve to §27.
